data.narrative:
Castelion is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Bryon Hargis, Sean Pitt, and Andrew Kreitz, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $100M in total capital raised. The relevant peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies across the Defense & Security sector: five private peers valued between $1.0B and $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Defense Unicorns, and Heven AeroTech) and two public comparables (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, yielding $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage, same-sector private comparables produced a median valuation of $1.4B, with observed range of $1.0B to $1.5B. These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $1.2B to $2.2B, with a median estimate of $1.6B.

The stage-progression method sits at the higher end, reflecting anticipated value creation through the next financing round. The peer-median result anchors to observable market transactions and is less dependent on step-up assumptions. Key uncertainties include the trajectory of capital deployment against market adoption milestones and relative positioning versus the identified peer set. The $1.6B median represents fair value given current stage and comparable evidence.
